Plainsight  

Description

1: See Invisible (1)
2: Ultravision

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 100 Skill: Divination
Casting Time: 5 Recast Time: 1.5
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Range: 0
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Deletable: No Blockable: Yes
Focusable: Yes Dispellable: Yes
Interruptable: Yes Short Buff Box: No
Target Type: Self Spell Type: Beneficial
Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: Your eyes tingle.
Cast on other: Soandso 's eyes glow violet.
Effect Fades: Your plainsight fades.

Game Description

Focuses your vision, allowing you to see otherwise invisible creatures, and allowing greatly improved night vision.

Items with this effect

  • Apatite of Sight
  • Arantir's Circlet of Discovery
  • Armsmaster's Gloves
  • Beast Tamer's Gloves
  • Blindeye
  • Bloodflow of Sight
  • Boneflow of Sight

Add Image
Post Comment
Plainsight
QuoteReply
# Apr 03 2005 at 12:20 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591780143482
176 posts
See invis and ultravision, mmm. I play a druid sometimes and you get ultravision in wolf form. It's the best sight available in game. With almost total darkness you see as far away as possible in daylight. The colors look different, but you won't get limited distance like you do in other ways of sight. I have tried all vision types in game and ultravision is the best for seeing everything at all times. Dark elves get innate ultravision and enchanters can buff ultravision on other toons. If you really are curious ask an enchanter to buff ultravision on you sometime and run around in burning woods. The smoke won't block your view at all.
▲ Page top

See Invisible(2) ?
QuoteReply
# Feb 05 2004 at 3:00 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1690762641916
81 posts
Would anyone know the specific differences between the See Invisible(2)found as part of Plainsight, and the See Invisible(1) found as part of Truesight?

I'm curious as to the difference in effects where a ranger would be concerned. (Helm of the Tracker vs. Planestriders. I realize the other differences, but I wanted to know specifically about this.)

Edited, Thu Feb 5 15:00:40 2004
▲ Page top
RE: See Invisible(2) ?
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2004 at 3:34 PM Rating: Decent
dafdaf
View User Forum
Scholar
1,273 posts
I came here wondering the exact same thing. My hunch is just they just use different slots (which don't effect worn effects anyway).
▲ Page top
I dont like this, wish i can turn off the effect.
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2003 at 7:27 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591772986898
Scholar
22 posts
This is the same as turning your gamma way up. The dark becomes light purple. On my computer, everything becomes light purple when my enchanter puts on the Silken Augmenter's Cap. If i turn down the gamma, it still looks bad. If i want to take a picture, i have to take off my cap so the colors look nice.
